name: Shrimp-And-Chicken Curry
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Chopped fresh dill (OR 1 tbsp Dried dill)
Canned or Fresh (if unavailable, use all stock)
recipeInstructions:
Combine chicken stock and clam juice in a small pan or pot. Cover and bring to a boil over high heat.
In a Dutch oven or paella pan, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the sausages and sauté to render some of their fat, about 5-10 minutes. Pour off all but 2 tablespoons of fat and return the pan to the stove.
Add the diced onions to the sausages and sauté, without browning or coloring, until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the rice and toss with the remaining fat from the sausages. Add the stock mixture, jalapeno peppers, curry powder, saffron, coriander, fennel, and cumin. If using dried dill, add it now.
Place the chicken, skin-side up, on top of the rice. Cover the casserole dish and bake at 375°F for 15 minutes.
Add the coconut milk or extra stock, shrimp, clams, and mussels. Return to oven, covered, and bake another 15 minutes or until the rice is tender and the shells have opened.
Sprinkle with fresh dill. Arrange on a platter or serve from the pan. Offer Indian condiments such as lemon pickle, garlic pickle, or papaya chutney.
